summary: First full-pass testing of Flystar Ops Portal. Backend pytest suite (27 tests covering auth, RBAC, shipment lifecycle, admin analytics, user mgmt) PASSED 100% after a trivial test-side email-case adjustment. Frontend Playwright E2E covered admin/employee/customer logins, customer self-signup, full pickup creation (auto AWB FLY...), redirect to shipment detail, employee queue with Accept buttons, admin command center with KPIs and 4 recharts, admin shipments page (search/filter/delete) and admin users page (add staff). All role-based redirects, RBAC (customer 403 on /api/users and /api/analytics/overview, employee 403 on DELETE shipment), public AWB tracking, and pricing engine work correctly. No blocking bugs found.

minor.issue: allow_origins=['*'] paired with allow_credentials=False is fine for the current Bearer-token flow, but the backend ALSO sets httpOnly cookies on login/register/refresh. Those cookies will never be sent cross-origin from the preview/prod domain because allow_credentials=False. Effectively the cookie path is dead code; only the Bearer-token in localStorage path is exercised. Either flip to allow_credentials=True with an explicit origin, or remove the set_cookies()/refresh-cookie code to simplify.

issues: React Router v7 future-flag warnings in console (v7_startTransition, v7_relativeSplatPath) - cosmetic only, not user-facing

action_items: Optional: decide if you want cookie-based auth (then set allow_credentials=True + explicit CORS origin) or purely Bearer (then drop the set_cookies/refresh-cookie logic).
Optional: opt-in to React Router v7 future flags to silence console warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 696 lines - close to the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routers (auth, users, shipments, analytics) before it grows further.
shipment_to_dict mutates the input dict (uses .pop on _id). Safe today because callers immediately return, but fragile if reused.

/api/shipments list with `q` parameter overwrites $or for employees (the employee role's $or scoping is lost when ?q= is sent). Low-impact (admin filter UI is the main consumer) but role isolation could leak unassigned shipments to an employee searching - actually currently safe since employee + q would still match query, but worth refactoring to compose role filter with $and.
On register, the new user's password is not re-validated for strength beyond min_length=6. Consider stronger password policy.
Brute-force lock_until check only blocks when rec exists for that ip:email combination - a distributed attacker can bypass.
All endpoints look healthy with proper Pydantic validation and HTTP status codes.
test_credentials: admin@flystar.in/Admin@123, employee@flystar.in/Employee@123, customer@flystar.in/Customer@123 - all verified working
seed_data_creation: Pytest run created: 1x customer signup user (TEST_signup_*), 1x secondary customer (TEST_other_*), 2x customer shipments (FLY...), 1x admin-created shipment (deleted in test), 1x admin-created staff (deleted in test). UI run created 1x more customer shipment via Demo Customer and 1x signup user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is a complete regression suite (27 tests, all green) covering: health, all 3 logins, bad-creds 401, /auth/me with Bearer, self-register, /quote, full shipment lifecycle (create -> employee accept -> waybill -> dispatched -> in_transit -> out_for_delivery -> delivered), public AWB track, customer/employee RBAC negatives, admin analytics/users/CRUD, admin shipment PATCH and DELETE. Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Frontend Playwright flow tested admin dashboard with KPIs+4 recharts, admin/shipments with delete/search, admin/users with add-user, customer new-pickup -> /portal/shipment/{id} redirect with AWB displayed, employee /queue with Accept buttons, /signup -> /portal/customer redirect.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Initial test failure on TestAuth.test_customer_self_register was a test-side issue: server correctly lowercases emails (per the email = payload.email.lower() in /api/auth/register), but the test asserted equality with mixed-case input. Test was corrected to assert against email.lower().
